stories.title: Giannis Trade Endgame — Heat vs. Celtics, 3 Days to Draft Night
stories.angle: Three days until the NBA Draft (June 23 at Barclays Center). The Giannis Antetokounmpo sweepstakes has narrowed to Heat vs. Celtics. There is reportedly an "issue" with Miami's package (Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jaquez + No. 13 pick). Celtics' Jaylen Brown package remains on the table and Giannis could extend his contract if he joins Boston. Bucks want deal done before draft night. Marc Stein: trade "drawing near."

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: 🚨 Giannis trade update: There's reportedly an "issue" with the Heat's offer. Celtics' Jaylen Brown package still on the table — and Giannis could sign an extension if he joins Boston. Draft night is the Bucks' deadline. Three days. #Bucks

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Heat vs. Celtics. Bucks want a deal before draft night (June 23). Marc Stein says it's "drawing near." The NBA offseason's biggest story resolves in 72 hours. 👀 #NBA

article.headline: The Giannis Trade Endgame: Heat vs. Celtics With Three Days to Draft Night

- [HIGH] Giannis sweepstakes now down to Heat vs. Celtics — Knicks officially out
- [HIGH] Heat offer package: Tyler Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jaime Jaquez Jr. + No. 13 pick
- [MEDIUM] There is a reported "issue" with the Heat's offer that Bucks have not resolved
- [MEDIUM] Celtics package centers on Jaylen Brown; Giannis could sign contract extension if he goes to Boston
- [MEDIUM] Marc Stein reports trade "drawing near" — Bucks want deal before draft night
- [MEDIUM] Giannis still prefers Miami per multiple reports
**Background:**
- Bucks went to June 23 draft as internal "deadline" per reports
- Both teams offered lottery picks and young players
- Celtics: All-British connection — Hamilton won Barcelona for Ferrari in analogous "unlikely winner" narrative

stories.title: NBA Draft Preview — 3 Days Out, Dybantsa Lock at No. 1, Peterson at No. 2
stories.angle: June 23-24 at Barclays Center in Brooklyn. AJ Dybantsa (-450 odds) projected No. 1 to Washington Wizards. BYU freshman averaged 25.5 PPG, 6.8 RPG, 3.7 APG in 35 games, shooting 51%. Darryn Peterson medicals clear — projected No. 2 to Utah Jazz. Cameron Boozer (23 PPG, 11 RPG, 4 APG at Duke) projected No. 3. Draft order: Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies, Bulls.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: AJ Dybantsa this season at BYU: 25.5 PPG, 6.8 RPG, 3.7 APG, 51% FG. He's the -450 favorite to go No. 1 to Washington on Monday. Three days until Barclays Center. #NBADraft

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: 2026 NBA Draft top 3: No. 1 Dybantsa (Wizards), No. 2 Peterson (Jazz — medicals clear), No. 3 Boozer (Grizzlies). Monday night at Barclays can't get here fast enough. #NBADraft

stories.title: Austin Reaves — Countdown to Decision, Lakers Remain Favorites
stories.angle: Reaves will decline his $14.9M player option by June 29. Lakers can offer 5yr/$239M max; rival teams capped at 4yr/$178.5M. Nets backing away as credible threat. Key factor: Reaves' decision "will not come down solely to a dollar figure" — his partnership with Luka Doncic and love of LA expected to keep him. Averaged 23.3 PPG last season.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Austin Reaves declines his $14.9M option by June 29. Lakers can offer 5 yrs/$239M. Other teams max out at 4 yrs/$178.5M. His Luka partnership + love of LA make this feel like a formality. #LakeShow

stories.title: Knicks Offseason — What Comes After the Parade?
stories.angle: Confetti settled on Broadway Thursday. Now what? Knicks front office faces a compressed offseason: draft is June 23 (they hold the No. 17 pick), free agency opens June 30. Officially out of the Giannis race. Focus shifts to protecting the roster: Brunson extension, OG Anunoby, Towns re-signing. Is this core capable of a repeat?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Confetti swept. Knicks offseason begins. Draft pick No. 17 (June 23). Free agency opens June 30. Brunson extension. OG Anunoby. KAT. Defending a championship starts now. #NewYorkForever

stories.title: Ja Morant Trade Market — Memphis Still Waiting on Giannis First
stories.angle: Memphis Grizzlies holding off Morant trade talks until the Giannis situation resolves (June 23 draft night). Heat remain interested in Morant as Plan B if Giannis stays in Milwaukee. Multiple teams circling. Could trigger the second major post-June-23 trade wave. Morant averaged 24.4 PPG last season. Memphis has No. 3 pick (Grizzlies) to use as leverage or package.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Memphis won't open the Ja Morant trade market until the Giannis deal closes. If Heat miss on Giannis, Morant is reportedly their Plan B. The second NBA domino falls Monday night. #Grizzlies
